9f3f153d101b.title: Get your menu online — 77% of diners check before visiting
9f3f153d101b.description: No online menu found. Most diners check the menu before committing. Without one, you lose the customer at the decision point.
9f3f153d101b.management_implication: Every day without a menu is a percentage of potential customers lost.

a547ec5afb90.title: Close the hygiene gap — 3 to 5
a547ec5afb90.description: An FSA rating of 3 is visible on your Google listing and the FSA website. Customers see it before they see your menu. Address the inspector's specific findings.
a547ec5afb90.expected_upside: Remove the trust ceiling on your overall score

10be4cb20d10.title: Establish basic web presence — you don't exist online beyond Google
10be4cb20d10.description: No website, Facebook, or Instagram detected. Even a basic page improves discoverability and gives customers a reason to trust you before visiting.

be0c588e0112.title: Fix the digital shopfront — you're losing walk-ins
be0c588e0112.description: You deliver a strong experience but your online presence doesn't make it easy to act on. Can a new customer confirm your hours, see your menu, and book — all within 60 seconds on their phone? If not, that's your fix list.
be0c588e0112.management_implication: Interest without conversion is vanity traffic.

08fdec8fc989.title: Strong rating masks weak competitive position
08fdec8fc989.description: Your 4.6/5 Google rating feels dominant but you sit below the local median (P0.0). Peer venues achieve similar or better ratings — in this market, 4.5 is table stakes, not a differentiator. Don't mistake the number for an advantage.
08fdec8fc989.management_implication: The rating looks good in isolation but not in context.

f05313da838e.title: Ranked #11 of 11 locally — peers are ahead
f05313da838e.description: At percentile 0.0, customers in this area have higher-rated alternatives. This isn't a crisis but it means you're competing on convenience rather than reputation.

95a43337cb6b.title: Guest experience trailing — investigate what peers do differently
95a43337cb6b.description: You trail peers by 1.8 points on experience. This isn't a label problem — it means customers are getting a measurably better experience on this dimension from your competitors.

deb5044f9a03.title: Compliance record weakest in peer set — creates inspection exposure
deb5044f9a03.description: You trail peers by 2.5 points on trust. This isn't a label problem — it means customers are getting a measurably better experience on this dimension from your competitors.

3bd66fc40ae3.title: Accelerate review volume — you're credible but not dominant
3bd66fc40ae3.description: At 144 reviews, your rating is stable but not commanding. Venues with 200+ reviews rank higher in Maps. A post-visit review prompt would compound monthly.
